title: thymus serpyllum f. pallens
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. pallens, also known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herb with tiny, grayish-green leaves and small, white or pale pink flowers. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Thymus serpyllum f. pallens has been traditionally used to promote relaxation, improve sleep, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herb in herbal remedies. It can be consumed as a tea, added to salves and ointments, or used in aromatherapy to reap its health benefits.
